headers.X-Payment-Required: x402; network=solana-mainnet; asset=usdc; amount=0.05; recipient=<provider_pubkey>; nonce=<server_nonce>

body.narrative: AgentMail provisioned a dedicated inbox address backed by an x402-priced inbound + outbound gateway. For neobank KYC flows the recommended integration is to spin a fresh inbox per customer-onboarding session, route the verification email through it, and tear the inbox down once the verification token has been claimed. Inbound retrieval polls every 5 seconds against the agent-scoped mailbox, returning structured JSON for the message envelope, headers, and body. Latency for receipt of typical 6-digit OTPs from KYC vendors averaged 8.2s end-to-end during AgentMail's reference benchmarks. Edge cases observed during integration: some KYC providers (notably Onfido and Persona) deliver verification links rather than codes, so the inbox consumer must extract anchor hrefs from the HTML body rather than regex-matching against the plaintext part. Spam-folder routing is not a concern because the agent-mail subdomain is not enrolled in the typical mailbox-provider reputation graphs. For high-volume neobank onboarding, batch inbox creation rather than serializing it — the API tolerates 50 inboxes-per-second comfortably and amortizes the x402 settle-to-create overhead across a session.
